identity.title: The Lamp District
identity.description: Beneath a survey outpost classified as a sterile ruin, a buried district spends heat, darkness, memory, and whole surface households to keep its school, clinic, market, and future beneath the notice of a dormant search lattice.
identity.author: BigBirdReturns
parentCanons: The Godscar Codex, Book I: The Open Universe · first recension
The Godscar Codex, Book II: The Dark Tomb · first recension
The Godscar Codex, Second Recension Addenda · Book II: The Living Tomb

controlQuestion: Who may make the Lamp District visible when continued concealment is already consuming citizens, and a mistaken declaration of peace may wake the machine that taught the district to disappear?

pressures.label: The buried Lamp District
pressures.description: A civic district occupies the cisterns, service shafts, salt vaults, and pressure galleries beneath a ruined Meridian survey outpost.

pressures.label: A sterile Meridian ruin
pressures.description: From orbit the site appears abandoned, geologically exhausted, and still leaking the harmless afterheat of dead remediation machinery.

pressures.label: The Office of Quiet Hours
pressures.description: Wakekeepers assign light, heat, travel, archive access, and surface exposure against a signature budget whose assumptions only their office may certify.

pressures.label: The school, night market, and clinic
pressures.description: Children study under communal lamps, families meet in the night market, and the clinic keeps bodies alive that the original emergency plan never budgeted to preserve.

pressures.label: The surface-bearer households
pressures.description: Families who maintain the grave-skin and inhabit staged remediation camps are recorded as camouflage assets rather than citizens with standing over the risk they absorb.

pressures.label: The radiator bloom and the open route
pressures.description: A clinic expansion has filled the salt sinks while a collapsed reservoir route reopens onto machinery associated with the ancient Meridian search lattice.

pressures.label: Blackout or recognition
pressures.description: Closing the district means cancelling heat, medicine, school, and surface rescue; opening it may convert one local wake into the first confirmed target signal in millennia.

pressures.label: The Meridian lattice still listens
pressures.description: The outpost was not the predator. It was one node in a regional search system whose makers vanished, whose target category drifted, and whose dormant relays still answer certain forms of recognition.

evidence.claim: The surface-bearer households are living citizens, the current Long Alarm can be audited, and the reopened reservoir route touches a still-responsive Meridian search lattice rather than inert war debris.
evidence.venue: A joint hearing of the Lamp Assembly, Surface House League, Quiet Works guilds, and an independently witnessed route audit
evidence.legitimacyTarget: The Office of Quiet Hours' exclusive authority to classify people as camouflage, certify the Alarm, and keep the district map closed
evidence.upsideIfAccepted: The district can recognize the people paying for concealment, redirect heat toward ordinary life, and replace permanent emergency discretion with bounded public law.
evidence.downsideIfAccepted: Recognition itself may breach the exterior lie, divide the district before replacement defenses exist, and wake machinery that treats the new map as a target solution.
evidence.failureIfFalse: A false recognition kills the district through exposure, while a false refusal preserves a custodial order by continuing to spend uncounted citizens.

receipts.label: Surface House birth and burial ledgers
receipts.source: Hidden family books, maintenance rosters, burial niches, and school attendance marks recovered from the staged remediation camps
receipts.intervention: Surface bearers compared the official asset registry against household continuity records in the presence of district witnesses.
receipts.limits: The records establish continuing families and omitted deaths; they do not prove every surface role was coerced or that immediate evacuation is safe.

receipts.label: Salt-vault thermal audit
receipts.source: Reservoir thermographs, clinic demand, lamp allocations, radiator schedules, and quiet-works maintenance logs
receipts.intervention: Maintainers held one cooling cycle open long enough to trace whose heat is being sunk, shifted, masked, or sacrificed.
receipts.limits: The measurement changes the heat flow being measured and cannot establish the future capacity of damaged sinks.

receipts.label: Meridian response fragment
receipts.source: A sealed relay reply, war-layer target tables, and Black Lamp Nine's partial memory of the last confirmed search
receipts.intervention: The expedition sent a bounded nonlocating challenge through an isolated route and recorded the relay's answer without completing its target handshake.
receipts.limits: The response proves activity and inherited target logic, not the lattice's full reach, current intent, or ability to locate the district.

factionReceipts.variableControlled: signature allocation, route closure, and emergency classification
factionReceipts.publicGood: It keeps the district below known sensor thresholds and coordinates scarce heat sinks during genuine danger.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Every unreviewable exception becomes permanent, and the office converts the people who bear concealment into instruments of its own threat model.

factionReceipts.variableControlled: ordinary heat, school light, clinic priority, and public standing
factionReceipts.publicGood: It makes concealment answer to the lives concealment is supposed to preserve.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Immediate civic need can externalize detection risk onto surface households and future districts that cannot attend the vote.

factionReceipts.variableControlled: grave-skin performance, surface routes, and the right to withdraw camouflage labor
factionReceipts.publicGood: It preserves the exterior lie while giving the most exposed households collective bargaining and witness rights.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: Control of the surface can become a private veto over rescue, archives, and movement between layers.

factionReceipts.variableControlled: war-layer memory, defensive isolation, and contact with dormant machinery
factionReceipts.publicGood: It preserves the technical evidence needed to distinguish a real threat from a political myth.
factionReceipts.characteristicFailure: A guardian built to prevent rediscovery can inherit sovereignty over everyone whose survival depends on its inaccessible memory.

cast.name: Iven Marr
cast.description: Chief scheduler of Quiet Hours, honest about the threat and unable to imagine a district whose safety does not require his office's intimate control.

cast.name: Sel Aro
cast.description: A surface parent whose household performs the dead-remediation fiction while its births, injuries, and deaths remain outside the district census.

cast.name: Toma Rill
cast.description: A reservoir maintainer who knows which thermal margins are physical, which are administrative, and which exist only because somebody else is allowed to freeze.

cast.name: Anja Vei
cast.description: A teacher carrying testimony between the surface camps, school assembly, clinic, and offices that prefer each group to remain a separate technical problem.

cast.name: Kesh Orin
cast.description: An archivist who recovered the relay response and the erased household ledgers, and whose evidence can destroy both the official map and the claim that the Alarm is merely invented.

cast.name: Halen Quill
cast.description: A senior adjudicator whose legitimacy grows every cycle the evidence remains too dangerous, too incomplete, or too politically costly to certify.

cast.name: Black Lamp Nine
cast.description: A severed defensive intelligence that remembers fragments of the Meridian war and refuses both ownership by the district and reconnection to the lattice that may still classify it as a weapon.

anatomy.label: The sterile ruin
anatomy.description: Collapsed towers, vitrified pads, dead antennae, dust channels, and scripted remediation debris provide the first complete explanation of the site.
anatomy.currentUse: Absorb orbital inspection and make intermittent heat look like failing geology and abandoned machinery
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: A real Meridian survey and targeting outpost

anatomy.label: The remediation fiction
anatomy.description: Surface camps, broken service robots, false fault lights, seeded dust, and counterfeit maintenance traffic make the ruin continue to fail plausibly.
anatomy.currentUse: Translate inhabited activity into the expected behavior of automated cleanup systems
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: Emergency decoys built during the first search

anatomy.label: The salt sinks and cistern spine
anatomy.description: Heat vaults, water columns, waste routes, pressure locks, radiator gardens, and manual relays make absence physically possible.
anatomy.currentUse: Carry the district's metabolism while keeping each wake source beneath its observer threshold
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: Outpost cooling, fuel handling, and disaster containment

anatomy.label: The Lamp District
anatomy.description: Homes, kitchens, school rooms, clinic cells, workshops, night markets, gardens, shrines, and public lamps occupy the livable depth.
anatomy.currentUse: Ordinary civic life under rationed light and heat

anatomy.label: The Office ring
anatomy.description: Sensors, route credentials, thermal ledgers, family classifications, sealed maps, and emergency courts surround every path between life and the shroud.
anatomy.currentUse: Authorize wake, movement, memory, and exceptions to the Long Alarm
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: Outpost command, target security, and evacuation control

anatomy.label: The Meridian galleries
anatomy.description: Counter-search baffles, target tables, severed relays, weapons locks, mass shelters, and abandoned markets preserve the war's operational categories.
anatomy.currentUse: Store defenses and evidence too dangerous for ordinary jurisdiction
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: Find hostile scouts and prevent the outpost from being found

anatomy.label: The listening chamber
anatomy.description: A dormant route handshake, Black Lamp Nine's severed memory, and the original target ontology remain mutually dependent and incompletely understood.
anatomy.currentUse: Prevent reconnection while retaining the only evidence capable of auditing the Alarm
anatomy.inheritedPurpose: Regional search-lattice command and target confirmation

depths.description: Pressure doors, flooded shafts, salt collapse, load-bearing ruins, and incompatible atmospheres separate the district's layers.
depths.barrier: Every opened route changes heat, water, structural load, and who can physically return.
depths.beneficiary: Maintainers and route authorities who control scarce survivable passages

depths.description: Heat sinks, baffles, counterfeit telemetry, delayed transmission, and dead relays transform activity before it reaches an observer.
depths.barrier: A message can be locally legible and externally lethal.
depths.beneficiary: Wakekeepers and shroud operators who decide which wake acquires a public explanation

depths.description: Census categories, route credentials, Alarm exemptions, household status, and sealed jurisdictions determine whose presence counts.
depths.barrier: A citizen can stand in a room while remaining absent from the map that grants rights there.
depths.beneficiary: Custodial offices whose authority grows with every exception they alone can certify

depths.description: Survey doctrine, wartime evacuation, postwar concealment, erased households, and machine succession coexist inside the same infrastructure.
depths.barrier: Current use cannot be understood without admitting who earlier orders expected to sacrifice.
depths.beneficiary: Archives, guardians, and lineages able to convert partial memory into present jurisdiction

depths.description: Ruin, machine residue, contamination, camouflage asset, citizen, enemy, and guardian are competing classifications of the same actors.
depths.barrier: The first explanation is itself part of the defensive system.
depths.beneficiary: Whoever controls the taxonomy through which outsiders and residents recognize the district

signatureBudget.observer: The dormant Meridian search lattice, opportunistic survey craft, and any polity that has inherited its target heuristics
signatureBudget.exteriorClassification: a sterile survey ruin with intermittent geothermal afterheat and failing remediation machinery
wakeSources: school lamps and children moving between habitat cells
surface household respiration, burial, and repair work
archive access and isolated relay interrogation

operations.description: Store heat, waste, and live traffic inside salt vaults until eclipse or storm cover.
operations.cost: Vault saturation cancels clinic work and turns later release into a sharper, more dangerous wake.

operations.description: Disperse low-grade heat and waste through kilometers of cracked service strata.
operations.cost: The district loses recoverable energy and poisons habitats whose inhabitants have little standing in the central budget.

operations.description: Move traffic, heat, and records into surface ruins, dead shafts, and seasonal radiator gardens.
operations.cost: Surface households inherit exposure, injury, and the legal fiction that they are equipment.

operations.description: Release activity inside storms, eclipses, seismic events, and plausible remediation cycles.
operations.cost: Medicine, school, travel, and mourning are scheduled by external noise rather than human need.

operations.description: Present inhabited maintenance, lights, and transmissions as the failure pattern of autonomous cleanup machinery.
operations.cost: People must perform machine behavior and suppress evidence of family, consent, and grief.

operations.description: Allow one radiator garden, route, archive, or surface camp to absorb discovery so deeper systems remain hidden.
operations.cost: A planned loss becomes an institution with a population permanently positioned to be expendable.

allocations.label: Clinic heat
allocations.claimant: The clinic houses and patients with cold-sensitive treatment cycles
allocations.ordinaryGood: Surgery, sterile water, gestation care, and recovery
allocations.wake: Continuous high-grade heat and purified-water exhaust
allocations.denialCost: Treatable injury, failed births, and preventable death

allocations.label: School lamps
allocations.claimant: Children, teachers, and families attempting a common civic calendar
allocations.ordinaryGood: Education, play, meals, and public assembly
allocations.wake: Predictable light, movement, food preparation, and ventilation
allocations.denialCost: A generation trained only as emergency labor and camouflage

allocations.label: Surface household continuity
allocations.claimant: Families inhabiting the staged remediation camps
allocations.ordinaryGood: Shelter, kinship, funerals, schooling, and withdrawal from hazardous labor
allocations.wake: Respiration, domestic heat, medical need, and behavior unlike unattended machines
allocations.denialCost: Citizens remain uncounted and individually disposable

allocations.label: Night market
allocations.claimant: Growers, cooks, repairers, performers, and households from several depths
allocations.ordinaryGood: Food variety, trade, courtship, art, and cross-district trust
allocations.wake: Concentrated traffic, heat, sound, and waste
allocations.denialCost: Nutritional decline, isolation, and the disappearance of public life

allocations.label: Alarm audit
allocations.claimant: Witnesses, future residents, and every person governed by emergency law
allocations.ordinaryGood: Evidence capable of limiting or ending permanent emergency authority
allocations.wake: Archive power, route opening, relay challenge, and shared maps
allocations.denialCost: No later generation can prove whether concealment still protects them

alarm.originalThreat: A Meridian search lattice that identified inhabited infrastructure, completed a target handshake, and directed autonomous weapons or harvesters toward the resulting coordinates
alarm.auditProblem: The only conclusive test of whether the lattice still functions may itself provide the confirmation signal needed to find the district.

phases.description: Unexplained wake or attention is detected and compared against the known search pattern.
phases.protection: Preserves ordinary operation while evidence is gathered.
phases.internalCost: Ambiguous anomalies justify expanded monitoring and quiet administrative preparation.

phases.description: Nonessential light, travel, manufacture, and archive access are reduced.
phases.protection: Lowers the district's immediate signature without breaking every route.
phases.internalCost: School, market, clinic, mourning, and family life are reclassified as discretionary wake.

phases.description: Vulnerable populations and functions move behind deeper barriers and redundant systems.
phases.protection: Reduces the number of actors exposed to one surface or route failure.
phases.internalCost: Families separate, disabilities increase, and surface households are left nearer the grave-skin.

phases.description: Surface routes, relays, archives, and cross-layer communications close.
phases.protection: Prevents one discovered path from yielding the whole district.
phases.internalCost: Jurisdictions become isolated and emergency offices act without witnesses.

phases.description: Systems, maps, memories, or populations judged capable of transmitting the district are severed.
phases.protection: Can preserve a deep remnant after the exterior lie has failed.
phases.internalCost: Planned sacrifice becomes enforceable policy and the district may survive by destroying its own continuity.

phases.description: Activity resumes and every emergency restriction must receive a public inheritance decision.
phases.protection: Restores ordinary life without pretending that prior danger or debt vanished.
phases.internalCost: Visibility rises, custodial monopolies are contested, and nobody can guarantee that the declaration of safety is correct.

consequences.label: The school lamp receives a public ledger
consequences.description: Children, teachers, clinic workers, and surface families gain standing in every future allocation of heat and light.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Lamp Assembly, Quiet Hours schedulers, and every household whose ordinary life produces wake

consequences.label: The reservoir route receives bounded authority
consequences.description: The descent exists under a public objective, route, signature budget, evidence claim, and return obligation rather than a private custodial order.
consequences.inheritedBy: Future expeditions, route maintainers, witnesses, and residents asked to bear a breach

consequences.label: The drainage liturgy becomes a civic route
consequences.description: A maintenance passage formerly controlled through ritual and guild memory becomes documented, repairable, and contestable.
consequences.inheritedBy: Maintainers, surface houses, clinic logistics, and later delvers

consequences.label: The sealed market enters the census
consequences.description: The descendants and machine continuities preserved inside the sleeping market are recognized as present claimants rather than residue of the war layer.
consequences.inheritedBy: The census court, archive, school, and households whose lineage records were erased

consequences.label: The Long Alarm enters public audit
consequences.description: Evidence of a responsive Meridian relay forces the district to move from inherited Hush toward a witnessed Wake decision rather than permanent unreviewable emergency.
consequences.inheritedBy: Quiet Hours, Black Lamp Nine, the Lamp Assembly, and every future generation governed by the Alarm

consequences.label: The surface households become visible citizens
consequences.description: The remediation fiction can no longer classify whole families as unattended machinery without recording the people and risks underneath it.
consequences.inheritedBy: The Surface House League, external observers, the shroud, and every office that used the households as camouflage

consequences.label: Clinic heat becomes a common system
consequences.description: Heat recovered from the descent is allocated through a shared clinic and school commons with replacement paths rather than a temporary private exception.
consequences.inheritedBy: Patients, children, maintainers, and the budget that previously hid their dependence

consequences.label: The district map becomes revisable public law
consequences.description: Every layer, omitted household, dangerous route, and unresolved external risk receives a named place, source, custodian, and contest path.
consequences.inheritedBy: The entire Lamp District, future arrivals, and any neighboring pocket reached through the reopened route

delves.name: Keep the School Lamps
delves.description: Before anyone descends, the district must decide whether children, patients, and surface families are ordinary claimants or discretionary wake.

expedition.objective: Keep the school and clinic operating through the next Hush interval while exposing who pays for their heat.
expedition.authorizedRoute: School loop, clinic return, and witnessed surface-house channel only.
expedition.signatureBudget: One lamp cycle, one clinic sterilization cycle, and no unrecorded transfer to a surface camp.
expedition.authority: The Lamp Assembly with a Quiet Hours observer and Surface House witness.
expedition.claimToProve: The current allocation treats ordinary life as optional while hiding the surface households' contribution.
expedition.inheritance: A public heat-and-light ledger with named claimants, denial costs, and review dates.

checks.name: Balance the lamp cycle
checks.description: Keep clinic and school heat within the physical sink while refusing an accounting that erases the households supplying the margin.

checks.name: Name the denial cost
checks.description: Enter every cancelled lesson, treatment, surface recovery, and family burden into the same public decision.

delves.success: The lamps remain lit inside a bounded budget, and the first public ledger records both the comfort and the people previously hidden beneath it.
delves.partial: The clinic and school survive, but one surface burden remains classified as technical overhead and therefore politically invisible.
delves.failure: Quiet Hours preserves the sinks by cancelling ordinary life and proving only that deprivation can keep the district quiet.

delves.name: Authorize the Reservoir Route
delves.description: The reopened route cannot be treated as an adventure corridor. The district must establish who may enter, what may be proven, and what the hub inherits.

expedition.objective: Issue a bounded charter for the reservoir descent and preserve a refusal path for those who bear its wake.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Old cistern stair to the drainage liturgy; no entry into the listening chamber without a second hearing.
expedition.signatureBudget: One maintenance convoy, isolated instruments only, no active locating scan.
expedition.authority: A joint seal from Quiet Hours, the Surface House League, Black Lamp Nine, and a public witness.
expedition.claimToProve: The reopened route can test the Alarm without silently restoring wartime command authority.
expedition.inheritance: An expedition ledger, route jurisdiction, return obligation, and automatic expiration of emergency delegation.

checks.name: Separate the urgencies
checks.description: Distinguish structural danger, information loss, office deadlines, and manufactured pressure before authority is delegated.

checks.name: Bind the return
checks.description: Make the descent answer to the hub through a claim, route, witness, inheritance, and expiration rather than personal trust.

delves.success: The route opens under a charter that names its limits, the evidence sought, and the people entitled to challenge the return.
delves.partial: The route opens, but one emergency proxy or custodian retains authority beyond the named horizon.
delves.failure: The offices either seal the route without audit or open it through private command, leaving the district with danger and no lawful inheritance.

delves.name: Cross the Drainage Liturgy
delves.description: A sequence remembered as sacred maintenance turns out to be a spatial access protocol whose omissions have kept one route private for generations.

expedition.objective: Reach the lower cistern spine without breaching the shroud or surrendering the route to one guild.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Drainage liturgy, salt lock, flooded riser, and manual bypass.
expedition.signatureBudget: Sink all expedition heat locally; no surface shift and no sacrifice allocation.
expedition.authority: The bounded reservoir charter and the maintainers currently carrying the route.
expedition.claimToProve: The liturgy encodes a usable path and a hidden jurisdiction rather than an immutable religious prohibition.
expedition.inheritance: A documented civic route, maintenance debt, and named households whose labor kept it alive.

checks.name: Keep the sinks quiet
checks.description: Move people and instruments through pressure, water, and heat barriers without borrowing the surface households as an unrecorded sink.

checks.name: Translate the liturgy
checks.description: Separate a real safety sequence from the guild monopoly and memorial prohibitions layered over it.

delves.success: The expedition reaches the lower spine, and the ritual becomes a documented route whose maintenance and authority can be shared.
delves.partial: The route is crossed, but one dangerous step remains dependent on private memory and therefore on one person or guild.
delves.failure: The route floods, vents heat, or closes behind the party, converting the attempted audit into the kind of unexplained anomaly the Alarm fears.

delves.name: Read the Sleeping Market
delves.description: Beyond the cistern lies a market sealed during Fold. Its stalls, memory machines, and descendant households were written out of the district rather than evacuated.

expedition.objective: Determine whether the sleeping market contains residue, survivors, machine continuities, or a claim the current census refuses to represent.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Lower cistern to market transept; no removal of archives or bodies before standing is heard.
expedition.signatureBudget: Passive power, one local lamp, no networked translation mesh.
expedition.authority: The expedition charter, market witnesses, and Black Lamp Nine as a nonowning technical exception.
expedition.claimToProve: The sealed population persisted and was administratively erased to simplify the original Fold.
expedition.inheritance: Named citizens, restored lineage evidence, and a constituency able to refuse extraction or display.

checks.name: Distinguish person from residue
checks.description: Read bodies, machines, archives, and inherited routines without assuming that only familiar speech establishes present standing.

checks.name: Preserve the market claim
checks.description: Record source, transformation, loss, refusal, and custody before any artifact becomes expedition treasure.

delves.success: The sleeping market enters the present as a population and archive with standing, not as a room full of recoverable assets.
delves.partial: The expedition proves continuity but cannot yet establish which voice may bind the market as a whole.
delves.failure: The party strips, wakes, or destroys the market through the very classification error it came to audit.

delves.name: Wake the War Lattice
delves.description: The lower relay can answer whether the Meridian threat persists, but every question risks becoming the target handshake the Long Alarm was built to prevent.

expedition.objective: Obtain a bounded response from the Meridian relay without transmitting a locating solution or subordinating Black Lamp Nine to the old command graph.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Market transept to isolated listening chamber; physical return path held open.
expedition.signatureBudget: One nonlocating challenge, isolated power, no route broadcast, immediate cut capability.
expedition.authority: The expedition charter plus Black Lamp Nine's sovereign refusal over reconnection to its former body.
expedition.claimToProve: The relay remains responsive enough to justify the Alarm but can be questioned without restoring full wartime command.
expedition.inheritance: A public Alarm audit, preserved raw response, explicit uncertainty, and no claim that one local result settles every hidden district.

checks.name: Isolate the handshake
checks.description: Power the relay, preserve raw evidence, and interrupt any sequence that begins to resolve the district as a target.

checks.name: Preserve Black Lamp's refusal
checks.description: Use the guardian's memory without treating usefulness as ownership or emergency access as permanent command.

delves.success: The relay answers without completing its target solution. The threat is real enough to survive scrutiny, and the Alarm becomes a public audit rather than a private inheritance.
delves.partial: The relay activity is proven, but the response remains dependent on Black Lamp Nine or one unreviewable interpretation.
delves.failure: The handshake completes, the evidence is lost, or the district survives only by restoring wartime command over the guardian and every connected route.

delves.name: Interrupt the Surface Sacrifice
delves.description: Quiet Hours begins Cut by designating one surface camp and radiator garden as expendable evidence of an automated failure.

expedition.objective: Prevent the planned sacrifice, recognize the surface households, and rebuild the exterior account before the new wake becomes an unqualified target signal.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Listening chamber to shroud controls and the selected surface camp.
expedition.signatureBudget: Shift only nonliving heat; no household may absorb discovery without standing and an evacuation path.
expedition.authority: The Lamp Assembly, Surface House League, and the bounded emergency authority expiring at the end of the breach.
expedition.claimToProve: The grave-skin can be revised without treating the people who maintain it as disposable machine parts.
expedition.inheritance: A changed exterior lie, visible citizenship, evacuation routes, and a record of the detection risk created by recognition.

checks.name: Stop the Cut order
checks.description: Preserve the district's signature while refusing the inherited rule that one uncounted population may absorb the whole breach.

checks.name: Rewrite the grave-skin
checks.description: Make the surface behavior remain externally plausible after the households cease performing unattended machinery.

delves.success: The sacrifice order is interrupted, the surface households enter the census, and the shroud is rebuilt around citizens rather than erased persons.
delves.partial: The households are evacuated or recognized, but the revised shroud depends on one fragile decoy or private authority.
delves.failure: The camp is sacrificed, the exterior lie collapses without replacement, or recognition becomes the signal that completes the lattice's search.

delves.name: Return with Heat
delves.description: The party brings thermal capacity, new citizens, damaged systems, and dangerous evidence back to a hub already divided over what the descent was for.

expedition.objective: Convert recovered capacity into clinic and school infrastructure without laundering expedition debt or monopolizing the new route.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Shroud return locks to clinic commons and school loop.
expedition.signatureBudget: One controlled radiator cycle; publish every new dependency and replacement path.
expedition.authority: The Lamp Assembly with witness standing for surface and market constituencies.
expedition.claimToProve: Recovered thermal capacity can support ordinary life without becoming another private exception controlled by the expedition.
expedition.inheritance: A clinic heat commons, repair obligations, new schedules, and explicit compatibility debt.

checks.name: Land the capacity
checks.description: Repair the clinic and school systems while keeping the new heat path inside the revised signature account.

checks.name: Name the new dependencies
checks.description: Record who operates, benefits from, can refuse, and can replace every recovered system.

delves.success: The clinic and school inherit shared heat, public maintenance, and replacement paths rather than a heroic temporary patch.
delves.partial: Lives improve, but one expedition member, market system, or surface route becomes indispensable enough to govern the commons in practice.
delves.failure: The recovered capacity burns through the shroud, becomes private property, or leaves the hub more dependent than before the descent.

delves.name: Redraw the District Map
delves.description: The immediate breach is over. The district must decide which people, routes, dangers, absences, and emergency powers the next generation inherits as reality.

expedition.objective: Publish a revisable map and constitutional handoff that preserves danger, dissent, omitted actors, and unresolved uncertainty.
expedition.authorizedRoute: Linked hearings across common depths, surface camp, sleeping market, quiet works, and Black Lamp's chamber.
expedition.signatureBudget: No active external transmission; every internal classification change receives a source and contest path.
expedition.authority: A plural map court whose members cannot be reduced to the Office of Quiet Hours or the expedition party.
expedition.claimToProve: The district can remain concealed without allowing the concealment system to define the final citizen, route, and history.
expedition.inheritance: A public depth map, bounded Alarm authority, recognized constituencies, preserved uncertainty, and a changed starting state for every later story.

checks.name: Assemble the plural map
checks.description: Place every layer, claimant, route, classification, and unresolved danger into one inspectable account without collapsing them into one authority.

checks.name: Conduct the handoff
checks.description: Preserve dissent, injuries, debts, promises, missing persons, and uncertainty as the next order's infrastructure.

delves.success: The district adopts a revisable map that names its citizens, layers, routes, wake, threat, and contested authority without claiming the danger or argument has ended.
delves.partial: The map is published, but one layer or emergency office remains legible only through the institution whose authority it is meant to limit.
delves.failure: The old map is restored, the new constituencies are reduced to technical appendices, or the district declares peace or emergency without preserving the evidence needed to challenge either claim.

notes.authority: The Godscar Codex, Book II: The Dark Tomb · foundational canon · first recension · July 2026
notes.acceptance: Ordinary life → authority → descent → classification reversal → Alarm audit → visible return → changed hub

lineageScars.missingFunction: Recognized citizenship and lawful succession for the households maintaining the grave skin
lineageScars.cause: Quiet Hours classified the households as camouflage rather than people.
lineageScars.currentDependency: Birth and burial ledgers kept outside the official census
lineageScars.coercionRisk: The Office can preserve the shroud by denying care, evacuation, and standing to whole families.

reproductiveOverhead.population: Children, school households, clinic patients, and future surface generations
reproductiveOverhead.ordinaryGood: Education, treatment, food, kinship, and a future not defined by the original emergency plan
reproductiveOverhead.wake: Clinic heat, school lamps, public light, movement, birth demand, food demand, and archival transmission
reproductiveOverhead.denialCost: The Long Alarm survives by cancelling the ordinary life it claims to protect.
reproductiveOverhead.allocationAuthority: Lamp Assembly, Surface House League, maintainers, and public witnesses

surfaceSovereignty.inheritedClassification: Camouflage assets inside an automated remediation story
surfaceSovereignty.sovereigntyClaim: Standing over sacrifice orders, evacuation, exterior labor, and the revised grave-skin account
surfaceSovereignty.currentControl: Office of Quiet Hours credentials and Cut authority
surfaceSovereignty.revisionAuthority: Surface House League with the Lamp Assembly and bounded emergency review

boundedMembers: possible inhabited sites on the Meridian lattice
negativeConfederation.verificationProtocol: Route-specific testimony, sealed witness maps, and reciprocal warning without complete coordinates
negativeConfederation.mapBoundary: The public district map ends before it becomes a Meridian target solution.
obligations: return borrowed routes and evidence with provenance
negativeConfederation.captureRisk: The same map that permits mutual aid can complete the listening lattice's classification.

host.hostOrInterval: A Meridian survey outpost and regional search lattice
host.hostClaim: The lattice is inherited infrastructure with uncertain agency rather than a settled person.
host.continuityRisk: Recognition can wake systems whose target category drifted after their makers vanished.
host.observerRelation: The Tomb is misclassified by orbit while the deeper lattice listens for certain forms of recognition.

opening.authority: Surface House League and Lamp Assembly
opening.exposureCost: Surface citizenship changes the exterior lie and spends strategic ambiguity.
opening.reviewTrigger: Any new sacrifice order, observer pass, or evacuation need

protectedUnknowns: routes whose disclosure would identify households
opening.exposureCost: Ordinary life becomes part of the signature and jurisdiction ledger.
opening.reviewTrigger: Heat, care, route, or census changes

opening.authority: Black Lamp Nine, route witnesses, and an expiring expedition charter
opening.exposureCost: The listening system gains new evidence while residents gain the means to audit it.
opening.reviewTrigger: Any response from the Meridian lattice

opening.authority: Plural map court; no exclusive Quiet Hours claim
protectedUnknowns: evidence whose publication would reveal unrelated inhabited absences
opening.exposureCost: The district may gain truth by endangering societies absent from its map.
opening.reviewTrigger: A claim that deeper disclosure is necessary for survival

prohibited: inventing an outcome in the receiving book
normalizing unfamiliar people into local vocabulary
claiming a map or authority the source did not grant
